name: Fertilizer / Urea
summary: Day 76 of Hormuz blockade. **C3 CF INDUSTRIES $125.50 (-3.8% 1D, +5.5% 1M) — REVERSAL Day 1, FERTILIZER-TRADE UNWIND**. Drivers (~80% crisis-trade-unwind): (1) PM Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address asked Indians to **cut fertilizer use 50%** = direct demand-shock signal for India's largest nitrogen import source; (2) Trump-Xi 'trade not war' implies Gulf urea/DAP supply normalization priced in; (3) Q1 EPS beat ($3.98 vs forecast +59%) already priced; stock fell 4.79% post-earnings (Apr) anyway. **Nutrien $71.29 (-1.0% 1D, -3.2% 1M, +2.8% 3M)** confirms broader fertilizer sector pressure but CF is cleaner Gulf-Iran-fertilizer cascade play. **CF +5.5% 1M / -3.8% 1D shows 2-session momentum compounding (May 12-13 +12.9%) IS REVERSING on Trump-Xi summit narrative.** Per markets dossier H reversibility map: CF $110-115 (-8-12% further) if MOU signs by May 21; **CF $145-160 (+15-27% snap-back) if Hormuz remains closed through June — Modi's fertilizer appeal needs actual behaviour change to validate demand-destruction thesis; if Indian wheat-sowing season demand persists, CF reverses upward**. **Urea (Middle East FOB) $552.50/mt May 12 (+0.91% d/d)** (TradingEconomics / Argus reference; up from January spike +$60/mt). Urea Granular FOB ME (futures) $765.00/mt = CME front-month, premium to spot reflects forward supply tightness. **CF/Nutrien equity weakness today is unwinding the FORWARD premium.** Qatar/Ras Laffan fertilizer/ammonia complexes (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients) curtailed since Feb 28; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected. World Bank 'fertilizer prices >+30% 2026' forecast carries forward. India 5-nation tour Modi flies UAE May 15 — energy/fertilizer diversification optionality. Wheat +2.7% 1D / +15.0% 1M; Corn +3.2% 1D / +6.7% 1M continue strong but driven by Black Sea more than fertilizer pass-through today. PRIOR (May 13): Modi five-nation tour CONFIRMED May 15-20 (UAE-Netherlands-Sweden-Norway-Italy); UAE leg explicit focus on 'energy security for India amid disruptions in global supply chains due to US-Iran war' — DIRECT GOVERNMENT ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF FERTILIZER/IMPORT-COST BINDING. Iran FM Araghchi arrives New Delhi May 13 BRICS, meets Modi May 14 = Modi-Araghchi-UAE narrative bracket is MOST-ACTIONABLE NARRATIVE BRACKET OF CYCLE for fertilizer. India publicly admitting it cannot solve import-cost problem; Modi flying to UAE means Indian state's first-best solution is bilateral Gulf supply — exactly the situation that keeps CF/Nutrien/BASF pricing power. Qatar/Ras Laffan offline since Feb 28; QAFCO/SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailment structural; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected. DAP India tender $935/t cfr May 11 = extraordinary print (pre-crisis $580-650); Urea US Gulf FOB futures +50% vs pre-war. CF YTD +37.7% 3M / +71% YTD (per UBS); UBS PT $97→$140; pricing-power capture confirmed in Q1 results ($615M earnings). **Corn +2.5% same session = fertilizer cost pass-through to grain producers visible.** Reversibility LOW: Qatar/Ras Laffan not coming back inside 60 days under any scenario. Cleanest medium-duration crisis-linked long. **Markets dossier E2: CF and Nutrien re-evaluated for first-class node promotion (CF already promoted May 12; Nutrien borderline — kept under monitor since 'second independent move' criterion not yet decisively met).** ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **CF INDUSTRIES +8.2% 1D TO $124.48 = MOVE OF THE DAY.** Sector regime shift: CF +8.2%, Nutrien +4.3%, BASF +3.5%, Linde +2.3% all bid together = NOT a single-stock event. **CAUSAL CHAIN (~85% crisis-linked): (1) Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address asked Indians to CUT FERTILIZER USE ~50% (mixed organic) — one of seven 'consumption-restraint' appeals; market reads this as Indian-government PUBLIC CONFIRMATION that fertilizer-import shock is BINDING, not hypothetical. (2) Qatar/Ras Laffan fertilizer/ammonia complexes (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients) curtailed since Feb 28 attacks; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected. (3) India's IPL DAP tender May 11 received offers at $935/t cfr east coast / $930/t cfr west coast — EXTRAORDINARY prints; DAP normally trades $580-650 ex-crisis. (4) Urea FOB US Gulf futures +50% since war began; UBS raised CF target $97→$140 explicitly noting 'may not fully reflect severity of ongoing industry disruptions.' (5) CF Q1 2026: net earnings $615M, EBITDA $983M confirm pricing-power capture; YTD shares now +71%.** Secondary contributor: Wheat +3.2% / Corn +3.2% same session = fertilizer cost pass-through to grain producers + tender activity. **This is a SUPPLY-DISRUPTION + DEMAND-CONFIRMATION confluence for the first time in cycle. REVERSIBILITY LOW: Ras Laffan not coming back inside 60-day window even under ceasefire; QAFCO production scarring structural; India's import bill binding through monsoon planting season (June-Aug).** The fertilizer trade is the cleanest crisis-linked long if Brent stays $100+ through Q3. **NEW PROPOSED EDGE: fertilizer-urea → india (existing edge, STRENGTHEN from weight ~5-6 to 7.5-8.0 on activation today via Modi address + DAP tender + US Gulf urea +50%).**

current.price: CF Industries $130.39 May 13 (+4.7% 1D Day 2; +12.9% cumulative over 2 sessions); Nutrien $71.98 (+1.0% 1D); Linde $503.87 (-0.1%); BASF €53.20 (-0.5%); DAP India CFR $935/t east / $930/t west (May 11 IPL tender lowest offers); Urea US Gulf FOB futures ~$547.50/t (+50% vs pre-war baseline)
current.delta_1m: CF +7.2%; NTR -3.2%; Linde -1.0%; BASF +1.4%; Wheat +13.0%; Corn +8.1%; Palm Oil +12.5%; Rough Rice +12.9%; Urea US Gulf +50% vs pre-war
current.structural_drivers: Qatar Ras Laffan offline = NOT 30-day disruption but structural input-cost reset (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailed); 35-40% of global urea export capacity affected; India's IPL DAP tender at $935/t = extraordinary (normal $580-650); Modi government PUBLIC CONFIRMATION of binding shock via consumption-restraint appeal; UBS CF PT $97→$140 with note that target 'may not fully reflect severity'; Reversibility LOW: not inside 60-day window; cleanest crisis-linked long if Brent stays $100+ through Q3.

active_triggers.condition: Urea price sustained above $800/mt for 4+ weeks
India urea stocks fall below 40 LMT by May 1, 2026
DAP India CFR remains above $900/t through end of monsoon planting (Aug 2026) — confirms structural input-cost reset
active_triggers.note: IPL tender May 11 received offers $935/t east / $930/t west — extraordinary prints. Trigger tests durability of fertilizer trade through monsoon.

active_triggers.condition: Indian fertilizer subsidy bill 2026-27 budget revision (rumored to overshoot ₹1.95 lakh cr) within 30 days — confirms binding fiscal cost on India

recent_signals.headline: CF -3.1% MAY 8 ON BLOWOUT EARNINGS — SELL-THE-NEWS AFTER +54% YTD RUN. CF Q1 EPS $3.98 vs $2.50 est (+59% surprise); revenue $1.99B vs $1.8B; net earnings $615M; EBITDA $983M; 99% available ammonia capacity utilization Q1. Urea Yuzhny FOB $616.25/T (+6.71% May 6, +15.12% MoM, +81.69% YTD); DAP $722.50/T (+11.58% MoM). **Hormuz handles ~35% of global seaborne fertilizer trade; Cape rerouting +10-14 days; China sulfuric-acid export suspension from May 2026 = structural phosphate bottleneck = nitrogen/phosphate fundamentals STRONGER than CF earnings showed.** Smart-money rotation: fertilizer-already-up (CF, NTR) → fertilizer-supply-chain (BASF +1.5%, Linde flat, Indian fertilizer producers as proxy). Most quantitatively interesting move on May 8 tape: signals fertilizer-Hormuz-cascade trade is TIRED at this entry level despite operational reality continuing to support higher prices.
sources: intel dossier Section E + market dossier C5
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED price tape multi-source; CF Q1 earnings = CONFIRMED via company release; structural drivers (Hormuz fertilizer share, sulfuric acid suspension, Cape rerouting) = CONFIRMED multi-source

recent_signals.headline: CF -6.5% / NTR -4.4% — APRIL 17 PLAYBOOK RERUN: MOU 'BOTH SIDES LIFT HORMUZ BLOCKADES' = QATAR FERTILIZER NORMALIZATION EXPECTATION = SCARCITY-PREMIUM UNWIND. CF -10.5% 1M now tracking symmetrical inverse of Hormuz-closure scarcity rally that drove it +27% in 3M. Urea generic $577.50/T (May 5) drifting lower; Argus Q2 forecast $715 has room to compress further; Egypt NCIC late-April spot $852/t = high-water mark. Second-cleanest peace-dividend trade after energy. If MOU signs: CF could give back another 8-12%. If Iran rejects: CF bounces +6-9% within 48hr.
